title: Items and Inventory — Data Structures
description: Create an Item class with name, item_type, value, and description attributes. Create an Inventory class that uses a list internally and provides add(), remove(), find(), list_items(), and total_value() methods. Create an ItemDatabase class that acts as a dict-based registry of all items by their name.
test_code:
sword = Item('Sword', 'weapon', 50, 'A blade')
assert sword.name == 'Sword', f"Item name should be 'Sword', got '{sword.name}'"
assert sword.item_type == 'weapon', f"item_type should be 'weapon'"
assert sword.value == 50, f'value should be 50'
assert sword.description == 'A blade', 'description mismatch'
potion = Item('Potion', 'potion', 25, 'Heals')
inv = Inventory()
inv.add(sword)
inv.add(potion)
assert len(inv.list_items()) == 2, f'Should have 2 items, got {len(inv.list_items())}'
assert inv.total_value() == 75, f'Total value should be 75, got {inv.total_value()}'
found = inv.find('Sword')
assert found is not None, 'find should return the Sword'
assert found.name == 'Sword', 'find should return correct item'
assert inv.find('Shield') is None, 'find should return None for missing item'
removed = inv.remove('Potion')
assert removed is True, 'remove should return True for existing item'
assert len(inv.list_items()) == 1, 'Should have 1 item after remove'
assert inv.remove('Potion') is False, 'remove should return False for missing item'
db = ItemDatabase()
db.register(Item('Axe', 'weapon', 40, 'Chops'))
assert db.get('Axe') is not None, 'Database should find registered item'
assert db.get('Axe').name == 'Axe', 'Database get should return correct item'
assert db.get('Missing') is None, 'Database get should return None for missing'
assert len(db.all_items()) == 1, f'Should have 1 item, got {len(db.all_items())}'
acceptance_criteria: Item class stores name, item_type, value, and description
Inventory.add() appends an item to the internal list
Inventory.remove() removes by name and returns True/False
Inventory.find() returns the Item or None
Inventory.list_items() returns the full list of items
ItemDatabase registers and retrieves items by name
hints: Item.__init__: just store all four attributes as self.name, etc.
Inventory.find: loop through self._items, return item if item.name == name
Inventory.remove: find the item first, then use self._items.remove(item)
total_value: sum(item.value for item in self._items)
